entry.title: Washington Post: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Dwindling resources may slow Russia, U.K. says; E.U. leaders meet on Kyiv’s candidacy
  • How a Russian investigative reporter found out he was a Kremlin target
  • Former Miss Brazil dead at 27 after having tonsils removed
  • A corruption inquiry ends in South Africa. Will justice follow?
  • Letters written, tanks in position as battle for Lysychansk looms
  • Maps of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine
  • Russia intensifies push to seize Luhansk
  • Pew poll: Global views on NATO and U.S. rise; for Russia, a decline
  • The scandal over Boris Johnson job-hunting for his wife won’t go away
  • Va. couple sailing to Portugal go missing after boat damaged in storm
  • In photos and videos: the aftermath of the deadly Afghanistan earthquake
  • Russia’s chokehold over gas could send Europe back to coal
  • E.U. poised to back Ukraine’s candidate status. Here’s what it means.
  • Russia makes ‘marginal gains’ near Severodonetsk
  • Afghanistan quake kills more than 1,000, injures 1,600, officials say
  • Two Jesuit priests in Mexico are slain in area torn by drug trade
  • Turkey’s Erdogan hosts Saudi crown prince, ending rift over Khashoggi murder
  • Microsoft launches effort to slow Russian propaganda on war, vaccines
  • Ukrainian photojournalist ‘executed in cold blood’ by Russians, group says
  • Under pressure from Beijing, Hong Kong’s schools become more patriotic
  • Survivors dig by hand after Afghanistan quake killing 1,000
  • 4 people wounded by man on stabbing rampage in New Zealand
  • N. Korea's talks of new army duties suggest nuke deployment
  • 'It's terrible:' Philadelphia mayor sorry for slain Filipino
  • Panama says more migrants crossing Darien Gap this year
  • Trumpet the bloodhound wins Best in Show at Westminster Dog Show
  • Uvalde schools police chief placed on leave as shooting inquiry deepens
  • Fear over recent shootings has some avoiding crowds, businesses
  • Andrew Gillum, former Fla. governor candidate, charged with wire fraud
  • Asian carp threaten the Great Lakes. Will calling them ‘copi’ help?
  • Officer faces probe for telling Black driver, ‘This is how you guys get killed’
  • When a call to the police for help turns deadly
  • South Dakota AG convicted on impeachment charges, removed from office
  • Minn. city to pay $3.25M in police killing of Daunte Wright
  • A Tesla was in a junkyard for three weeks. Then it burst into flames.
  • Tributes pour in for El Chango, the Mexican narco pet monkey shot dead
  • Theater backs off plan to fast-forward through same-sex ‘Lightyear’ kiss
  • In rare medical procedure, these two women share one liver
  • Cousins receive mystery postcards sent decades ago: ‘I was stunned’
  • Post Politics Now: With new evidence coming in, Jan. 6 panel to continue hearings in July
  • As Biden touts gas tax pause, even some of his own officials balk
  • W.Va. politician, abortion protester sentenced to 3 months for Jan. 6 role
  • Judge delays Proud Boys trial in fallout from House Jan. 6 hearings
  • Sen. Cramer says he may need part of finger amputated
  • DOJ wants to know if Sidney Powell is funding Oath Keepers’ defense
  • Stock recovery falters after Fed chief acknowledges recession risk
  • What a pause in the gas tax would mean for prices at the pump
  • Female scientists don’t get the credit they deserve. A study proves it.
  • Formula shortage adds to financial crunch for farmworker families
  • This inflation-proof bond is paying 9.62%. Here’s how to buy it.
  • The unintended consequences of the $178 billion bailout to keep hospitals and doctors afloat
  • Top Senate Democrat casts doubt on prospect of major data privacy bill
  • Computer chip subsidies face a make-or-break moment in Congress
  • Facebook Oversight Board says company should be much more transparent
  • Landover Hills is ‘a welcoming community’
  • Who is responsible for repairing damaged insulation in a condominium?
  • Housing market slowdown takes hold as mortgage rates dig in
  • Foreign Firms’ Russian Assets Are Lousy Deals for Locals
  • Waiting for a GSK Mega Deal Hurts Like Toothache
  • Crypto Is Not Too Big To Fail, Even With Help From FTX
  • Powell Finally Has Markets Right Where He Wants Them
  • Why Fracking Won’t Solve the Global Oil and Gas Squeeze
  • What’s a Reverse Currency War and Who’s Fighting One?
  • What ‘Friend-Shoring’ Means for Trade in a Less-Friendly World
  • A ‘Price Cap’ on Russian Oil — What Would That Mean?
  • How many Rusty Bowers Republicans are out there?
  • ‘Stakeholder’ capitalism is parasitic progressivism
  • The U.S. door swings open to Ukrainians
  • We need laws to protect election workers like ‘Lady Ruby’ Freeman
  • The voices the Jan. 6 committee needs to hear
  • Restricting U.S. oil exports would betray European allies and benefit Russia
  • These Republicans defended democracy. How many will be left after 2024?
  • D.C. voters sent mixed messages in the Democratic primary
  • A question for Garland: Do you know how it feels for POTUS to target you?
  • Buried in the gun deal is a significant advance
  • When even Caterpillar flees Illinois, you know the state is in trouble
  • Think state supreme courts will save abortion rights? Think again.
  • Virginia’s U.S. House elections are set
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: The Sydney Morning Herald: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald: 23 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-23 07:10AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Caro questions De Goey's ADHD revelation timing
  • Brisbane woman found tied up in her home
  • Australia news LIVE: Housing shortages could cost nation $25 billion a year; Crown Sydney wins conditional licence for Barangaroo casino; energy market suspension to be lifted
  • Barilaro’s US post is a bad look for NSW Coalition
  • Queensland considers new tourism tax
  • Trumpet ‘the underdog’ is first bloodhound to win Best in Show at Westminster
  • Father’s plea to change mental impairment defence
  • The new Minions is relentless. It will make your ears ring and your eyes pop
  • Melbourne Airport's busiest period in more than two years to begin tomorrow
  • Labor national secretary Paul Erickson meets China’s top diplomat, Xiao Qian
  • Best of cartoons, June 23, 2022
  • Senior Victorian ministers expected to announce retirement
  • ‘The million-dollar question’: Will FINA ruling impact community sports?
  • Social media warning amid community concern over WA paedophile’s release
  • ‘It blew up’: The day Kerevi apologised to Eddie
  • Mackay 'didn't believe anyone would kill him'
  • Deputy premier among four senior Victorian ministers set to retire
  • Sarah Schmidt’s first novel began with a dream of Lizzy Borden. The second was far more disturbing
  • Stranger Things bullying scene triggering for us ’90s kids
  • Blue moon: Why Tedesco’s shorts keep falling down
  • Consumers penny pinch on snacks as treasurer talks up economy
  • Wilkinson asked not to comment further on Higgins as new trial date set
  • Cartoons of the week
  • Stop playing dirty: Why Australian politics must clean up its act
  • Butler promises priority legislation to mandate aged care nurses

  • St Catherine’s parents enlist more schools to fight same-sex marriage statement
  • TikTok star admits defying COVID restrictions at anti-lockdown rally
  • Labor promises to dismantle state’s scandal-ridden rail corporation
  • Crowded classes: Schools promised four years ago yet to start construction
  • Teachers, nurses taken for granted again in NSW’s budget
  • In the Herald: June 23, 1956
  • We’re gonna party like it’s 1949!
  • NSW Labor promises Sydney Harbour Bridge, tunnel revenue for drivers
  • Perrottet distances himself from Barilaro’s appointment to New York role
  • Cyclists and pedestrians don’t want to share in Sydney - we want our own paths
  • NSW nurses and midwives to strike for third time this year
  • ‘He is going to fight to his dying breath’: Renowned swimming coach charged over alleged sexual assault
  • ‘Utter lack of remorse’: Feds say Ghislaine Maxwell ‘deserves at least 30 years’ in jail
  • Trump allies issued subpoenas in ‘fake elector’ probe
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • Multiple people injured in Auckland stabbing
  • Multiple people injured in ‘totally random’ Auckland stabbing attack
  • Afghan families were asleep when quake struck, now bodies pile on the streets
  • Jamaican sprints to depose Commonwealth baroness, says she’s no British puppet
  • At least 1000 people killed after Afghanistan earthquake
  • ‘Sexist, vulgar and abusive NASA,’ says former top female executive
  • Prince Charles in Rwanda pleads for no more genocides
  • ‘Clear setback’: Russia captures east Ukraine villages in key positions
  • Historic gun laws in the United States one step closer after Texas mass shooting
  • Death toll continues to rise after earthquake in eastern Afghanistan
  • Poliovirus detected in London sewage, national incident declared
  • Daunte Wright’s family to receive $4.6 million after police killing
  • China to have a bigger say in world affairs but must be transparent: Marles
  • Sri Lanka’s economy ‘has completely collapsed’, says despairing PM
  • Top Texas cop slams response to school shooting
  • ‘The Liberal Party can’t exist for itself’: Constance on life after politics
  • Australia's inventor of the bikini, Paula Stafford dies aged 102
  • Man claims injury compensation while earning $70K from employers
  • Kidnapping charges withdrawn, costs awarded to Mick Gatto’s nephew and two other men
  • Wildcats coach quits after just one season
  • ‘High-end criminal behaviour’: Police investigate spate of speed camera attacks
  • Apology, pardon for WA mother who ‘endured the unthinkable’
  • Queensland grandma assaulted, zip tied to furniture during 'distressing' break-in
  • Apology, pardon for WA Aboriginal mother who ‘endured the unthinkable’
  • War veteran lacked mental capacity to give house and fortune to hairdresser
  • COVID test sold in Woolies, Coles slapped with fine over failure to provide data
  • Samsung Galaxy S7 ads
  • Samsung fined over false water resistance claims for Galaxy phones
  • Mining companies accused of corporate failure in shocking WA FIFO sexual harassment report
  • EnergyAustralia racing to maintain Yallourn coal power supply
  • ‘The truth matters’: Rupert, Lachlan Murdoch dragged into $2.3b Fox News defamation suit
  • Mind the gap: The rich-poor divide on clean power is getting wider
  • The Wrap: ASX moves higher despite commodity prices hurting energy, miners
  • Milk run: Biden sends more planes to pick up Bubs Australia infant formula
  • Recession alarm bells are ringing as the war on inflation escalates
  • The man with the Midas touch who became the world’s richest person
  • Wasn’t Bitcoin supposed to be a hedge against inflation?
  • Turning the screw: Europe and Russia are both losing the financial war
  • 650 per cent surge: Revlon filed for bankruptcy, now it’s a meme stock
  • Rupert Murdoch and Jerry Hall set to divorce: NYT
  • Australian trading app Stake to allow users to lend US shares
  • Zip Co tries to reassure market as share price tanks
  • ‘No guiding strategy’: Former Judith Neilson Institute executive unloads
  • Call me frivolous, but is it possible Harry Styles might look my way?
  • Hello darkness, my old friend: Gothcore makes an unlikely return
  • Punk meets Broadway: what Hamilton’s Melbourne director made next
  • ‘Oh God, no chance’ ... but then a Tim Finn trio beat MGM to the punch
  • Parents discover their five-year-old daughter has a golf-ball sized brain tumour
  • Kurt Vonnegut’s story took 40 years to film. He probably knew it would
  • My Gawd! This 100-year-old etiquette guide might still hold some useful lessons
  • She hacked an old knitting machine to weave the universe into a work of art
  • 22-year-old Australian actor the star as HBO boosts Game of Thrones prequel
  • Rupert Murdoch and Jerry Hall to divorce
  • Leigh Sales faces her toughest (and funniest) interview yet ... with herself

  • Will it be Burning Love or Heartbreak Hotel for Elvis at the box office?
  • Delivery rider’s family wins landmark payout after death
  • National weather forecast for Thursday June 23
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Japan's SMFG to buy 10% stake in SBI worth over $580 million

entry.title: Guardian: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • UK borrowed £14bn in May as inflation drove up interest debt costs – business live
  • Dramatic rescue at world championships after swimmer faints and sinks to bottom of pool
  • Diego Maradona: eight to go on trial in Argentina for homicide
  • The House of Fortune by Jessie Burton review – a sequel to The Miniaturist
  • Russia-Ukraine war: EU leaders to decide on Kyiv bid; Russia aims to turn Donbas cities into a Mariupol, Zelenskiy says – live news
  • Rupert Murdoch uses London visit to try to boost ratings at talkTV
  • Rambo: First Blood review – Stallone’s 80s hero ripe for devotees of old-school action

  • 10 of Europe’s best destinations for LGBTQ+ travellers this Pride season

  • ‘People may be overselling the myth’: should we bring back the wolf?

  • The King of Kowloon: my search for the cult graffiti prophet of Hong Kong
  • Marseille, Alexandria and Istanbul prepare for Mediterranean tsunami

entry.title: Korea Herald: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 23 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-23 06:34AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Exclusive interview: Hong Kong’s leader-designate John Lee says ‘I am a pragmatic person’ and ‘I will be open to everybody’
  • BN(O) scheme: more than 8,500 Hong Kong students estimated to have applied for school places in Britain since September, according to UK report
  • Incoming Hong Kong leader John Lee promises more aggressive approach to countering ‘fearmongering and badmouthing’ by city’s critics
  • Incoming leader John Lee working on strategy to reopen Hong Kong border with mainland China, may reduce quarantine for arrivals
  • False Covid positives leave more than 130 people stranded overnight at Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge
  • Senior Beijing official with over 30 years’ experience in Hong Kong affairs appointed to deputy role in nation’s top advisory body
  • Microsoft supports Hong Kong start-ups in partnership with Science Park, Cyberport
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum can help foster sense of national identity among residents, outgoing leader says
  • Owner of Hong Kong’s iconic Jumbo Floating Restaurant could claim compensation if vessel had been insured, analysts say
  • ‘Glocalised’ school-based curriculum at Caritas Wu Cheng-chung Secondary School empowers innovation and inspires students from nearly 20 countries and regions
  • Hong Kong police arrest 7 for allegedly conspiring with 3 retail outlets to cash in HK$220,000 worth of e-vouchers
  • Hong Kong to include Korean in DSE university entrance exam from 2025 amid K-Culture boom
  • Hong Kong child abuse scandal: first of 27 employees charged sentenced to 4 months’ jail
  • Hong Kong’s incoming deputy justice secretary to quit pro-Beijing group amid concerns membership could undermine impartiality
  • Amid record-breaking floods in China, premier calls for stepped up disaster control and relief
  • Shenzhen tightens Covid-19 controls with 48-hour negative test result required to use public transport after three new cases found
  • China officials who abused health codes to stop bank protests punished
  • China can expect a ‘harder line’ from G7, Nato countries at summits
  • G7 pledges to counter ‘state-driven censorship’ and help protect journalists
  • Long Covid: children can have lingering symptoms but the chances are low, study finds
  • To keep up with China, Pentagon must cut red tape and embrace latest technology, top US official says
  • 6 ways China thinks it can offset zero-Covid impact to get its economy back on track
  • ‘It can create life’: Chinese team claim stem cell breakthrough in mice study
  • As China-Horn of Africa Peace Conference ends, Beijing’s envoy offers to play regional security role
  • Joe Biden’s trade chief Katherine Tai calls China tariffs ‘significant’ leverage
  • Chinese rights advocate Xu Zhiyong on trial for state subversion amid secrecy and tight security
  • US-China relations: Beijing has own ‘trade weapons’ to counter Xinjiang ‘forced labour’ law
  • Uneasy times for Hongkongers making their home in mainland China
  • At BRICS forum, China’s Xi Jinping takes aim at West over Russia sanctions
  • Taiwan navy helicopter makes hard landing, injuring four crew members
  • Bitcoin market meltdown prompts fresh warning in China that value of world’s leading cryptocurrency could fall to zero
  • China’s cash-strapped local governments accused of misusing funds as economic pressure grows
  • Tech war: Shenzhen recruits Japanese semiconductor heavyweight as China moves to challenge US, Korean dominance in memory chips
  • China’s Tangshan city stripped of ‘civilised’ status after brutal attack on women diners
  • China’s electricity demand soars as heatwaves bake northern, central provinces
  • China inflation: Li Keqiang says ‘prudent’ economic policy, bumper wheat harvest leaves room to tackle risks
  • US business group calls for ‘urgent’ talks on Taiwan free-trade deal
  • Inquiry finds Australian mining firms ignored ‘horrific’ sexual assaults against women
  • Malaysia’s palm oil output recovery hopes dashed as fertiliser, worker shortages persist
  • Are Singapore, Australia, Hong Kong expats still living the dream? Burnout, stress and loneliness rife since Covid-19, survey finds
  • How the US can help end Afghanistan’s humanitarian crisis
  • Japan raps China, Russia warships’ ‘show of force’ during Asean meeting
  • Indonesia deports Japanese Covid fraud suspect – US$7.3 million in government funds stolen
  • Cash-strapped Pakistan scores IMF financial aid win – but millions of people may face ruin, analysts say
  • Malaysia’s Ismail Sabri Yaakob unveils more handouts for low-income families as Najib, Anwar take aim at rising prices
  • Singapore’s Covid-19 infections rise amid new subvariants but no change to rules – although health ministry urges boosters
  • Myanmar minister joins Asean defence meeting as junta shifts Suu Kyi trial to prison
  • US$3 billion tax bill for Philippines’ Marcos family will be collected vows new chief – 30 years after dictator’s death
  • US bans use of anti-personnel landmines everywhere – except the Korean peninsula
  • Russian TV protester Marina Ovsyannikova in exile says caught in ‘information war’
  • Afghanistan earthquake: Rescuers dig by hand as UN, US and EU offer help
  • Ghislaine Maxwell deserves 30-55 years in prison: US prosecutors
  • Polio back in UK after virus detected in London sewage samples
  • Vienna returns as world’s ‘most liveable city’; Ukraine’s Kyiv not on list amid war
  • US Capitol riot hearings to take break as new evidence floods in
  • Russian troops ‘executed’ photographer in Ukraine, press group says
  • Russia’s Sergey Lavrov in Iran to discuss nuclear deal, cooperation
  • Rupert Murdoch and Jerry Hall are getting a divorce, report says
  • Türkiye’s Erdogan and Saudi crown prince embrace as years of rancour end
  • Coronavirus: French face Covid-19 upsurge, other Europeans see increase
  • Moscow accuses Germany of ‘Russophobic hysteria’ on date of Hitler’s invasion – while pounding Ukraine
  • Tesla battery supplier CATL raises US$6.7 billion in Shenzhen stock placement as mainland China’s capital market thrives
  • Alibaba, Geely lift Hong Kong stocks on China stimulus bets as Xi pledges to achieve 5.5 per cent growth target
  • China’s appetite for protein developed through fermentation grows, amid drive to boost demand for meat alternatives, net-zero goal
  • HKMA intervenes to defend Hong Kong dollar peg, taking currency purchases beyond HK$100 billion amid capital flight
  • Hongkongers spoilt for choice in Lohas Park, as developers offer lower prices for new projects to counter interest rate rises
  • Exchange Fund: how US$574 billion war chest defeated Soros, defended Hong Kong dollar peg in post-handover financial crises
  • Citigroup predicts 16 per cent upside in Hang Seng Index as corporate earnings, China vaccine progress revive market confidence
  • As China’s zero-Covid policy takes an economic toll, Hainan free-trade port could provide a boost
  • Shanghai Stock Exchange is world’s No 1 IPO venue as Chinese firms return home to counter US delisting threat
  • ESG is not a scam, but much work remains to define and standardise it
  • Hong Kong start-up EcoBricks, backed by Sino Group, upcycles ‘problem plastic’ to reduce landfill use and carbon emissions
  • Bitcoin resumes slide amid mounting concerns about recession
  • Amazon has a plan to make Alexa mimic anyone’s voice
  • Google faces second turnover fine in Russia over failure to delete banned content about Ukraine
  • How Hong Kong’s arts scene has changed in 25 years and the challenges to come as its cultural planning becomes more aligned with mainland China
  • The sinking of Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant – bane or blessing?
  • Quarantine spawned a cookbook, washouts bred dining pop-ups, and she launched a handmade sauce business in Hong Kong mid-pandemic – Simran Savlani has been busy
  • For Singapore’s fashion designers, post-Covid recovery rests on drawing customers from overseas
  • Apple M2 MacBook Pro 2022 13-inch review: new chip that’s super efficient takes laptop to the next level, even if it may look the same
  • Look Up movie review: bland and patronising stories of grown-up children chasing their dreams hardly make for a celebration of 25 years of Chinese rule in Hong Kong
  • The best seafood, tempura and casual French fare in Hong Kong – a wine expert’s picks
  • How reading The Art of Retail Buying taught co-founder of sustainable fashion label Allegory everything she needed to know about business
  • Inside Asia’s obsession with Boys’ Love TV shows: queer series like Thailand’s 2Gether and South Korea’s Semantic Error have millions of fans, social media groups, YouTube channels and LGBT podcasts
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Giorgio Armani mixed elegance with a touch of whimsy through colour blocking and surprising prints for spring/summer 2023 in shades of blue
  • Meet Jeon Jong-seo, Money Heist Korea’s rising star: she acted with Zac Efron and Kate Hudson, her first film was shortlisted for an Oscar before Parasite – and she’s dating her former director
  • STYLE Edit: Gucci’s Landmark Hong Kong flagship store unveils its swanky makeover to offer the Gucci DIY service, and an exclusive VIP lounge and bar for clients
  • British royals, undercover: Queen Elizabeth, Prince Harry, Meghan Markle, Prince William and Kate Middleton have all gone incognito – and Princess Diana even crashed a gay bar with Freddie Mercury
  • Who is Lady Sarah Chatto, Queen Elizabeth’s beloved niece? The low-key British royal is the daughter of Princess Margaret, and she attended the Platinum Jubilee with Meghan Markle and Prince Harry
  • Who is Elon Musk’s teen transgender daughter, Vivian Jenna Wilson? The 18-year-old twin is changing her name and gender – and does not ‘wish to be related to’ her billionaire father
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Fendi presented denim, fringe and rubber sliders for its spring/summer 2023 collection, with an earthy colour palette in abundance
  • Milan Men’s Fashion Week: Versace invoked pop Baroque style with a sustainable pull for its spring/summer 2023 collection, with Donatella Versace at the helm
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

  • VOX POPULI: Half-baked look at government’s COVID response led to bad report
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
